ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.
translated: Yakubu, the son of Isawu, is a farmer who lives in Idom.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli ti maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani ti, ka mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, niinsi, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu buŋkobiri zaa niŋi.”
translated: “My friend, I am telling you, if you see a woman who is beautiful and well-dressed, don’t be deceived by her appearance, because she may be a thief, a liar, or a witch.”

ref: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni nyaasi durinye niriba maa, ka ba zaa niŋini yabeniŋiniba.
translated: I am here, I will tell you the story of the ancestors, so that you will know their history.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Ibrahim ni Saara zaa daa n kurigini, haali, Saara daa n bi lee n pegira.
translated: Ibrahim is Saara's husband, but Saara wants to marry someone else.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Yakubu ni wumi n ŋa, o tolimi n yeli Rachel ni Liya, ni ba ta o saani, seela o ni buŋkobiri n be la.
translated: Jacob had two wives, Rachel and Leah, and he loved Rachel more than Leah.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.
translated: Yakubu, the son of Isawu, is a man of great strength, and he is a farmer in Idom.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli ti maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani ti, ka mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, niinsi, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu buŋkobiri zaa niŋi.”
translated: “My child, listen carefully, I will tell you a story. There was a time when the world was new, and the animals were all together, and they were all friends. They lived in peace and harmony.”

ref: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni nyaasi durinye niriba maa, ka ba zaa niŋini yabeniŋiniba.
translated: I am here, I am the one who will tell you the story of the ancestors, so listen carefully.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Ibrahim ni Saara zaa daa n kurigini, haali, Saara daa n bi lee n pegira.
translated: Ibrahim is Saara's husband, but Saara doesn't want him anymore.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Yakubu ni wumi n ŋa, o tolimi n yeli Rachel ni Liya, ni ba ta o saani, seela o ni buŋkobiri n be la.
translated: Jacob had two wives, Rachel and Leah, and he loved Rachel more than Leah.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.
translated: Yakubu, the son of Isawu, was a man of great strength and courage, and he was a leader among the Idom people.
